meta.title: About — the team behind CorpSec | CorpSec
meta.description: We turned 79 jurisdictions and 30+ licensed local partners into one back office — so founders stop guessing where to incorporate. Meet the people (and the AI) who run it.

hero.title: We made “where should I incorporate?” a two-minute question.
hero.sub: CorpSec turns 79 jurisdictions and 30+ licensed local partners into one back office. You tell us where — or let our advisor tell you. We handle the formation, the address, the filings and the accounting. One dashboard. One invoice.

story.heading: Picking the wrong jurisdiction is a six-figure mistake — we got tired of watching founders make it.
story.sub: The “right” answer depends on your investors, customers, bank and tax exposure — and the wrong one can stall a round or cost years to unwind. The advice was scattered across four advisors who each sold what they had. So we built the opposite.

fit.title: The fit, not the margin
fit.body: Our advisor ranks all 79 jurisdictions for your situation. We get paid the same wherever you land — so the recommendation is the one that’s actually right for you.

dashboard.title: One dashboard, one invoice
dashboard.body: Every renewal, filing and bookkeeping deadline is handled by licensed local partners and surfaced in one place. No chasing registries in languages you don’t speak.

licensed.title: Licensed, insured, accountable
licensed.body: Work is delivered by regulated corporate-service providers and accountants in each jurisdiction — professional-indemnity insured, not a forum thread and a prayer.

team.heading: The people who keep your company in good standing.
team.sub: Credentials first, personality second — but we’d be lying if we said the second part didn’t matter.

maya.name: Maya Chen
maya.bio: Eight years structuring cross-border holdings at a Big Four firm before deciding the spreadsheets had won. Started CorpSec so founders stop emailing four advisors to answer one question.
maya.fun: Can recite the corporate tax rate of any country you shout at her. Please don’t test this at parties.

daniel.name: Daniel Okonkwo
daniel.bio: Qualified solicitor who has filed in 14 jurisdictions and read more articles of association than is medically advisable. Keeps our jurisdiction data honest and current.
daniel.fun: Firmly believes a boring cap table is a beautiful cap table.

sofia.name: Sofia Marchetti
sofia.bio: Vets and manages the 30+ licensed local providers behind every filing — so you never have to cold-email a registry in a language you don’t speak.
sofia.fun: Maintains a personal vendetta against vague invoices.

arjun.name: Arjun Patel
arjun.bio: Built the dashboard that turns 30+ partners and a wall of deadlines into one calendar and one invoice. Spends his day removing steps from the flow.
arjun.fun: Measures success in deadlines you never had to remember.

lena.name: Lena Brandt
lena.bio: Keeps 500+ companies in good standing across every time zone. If a filing is due next month, Lena already knows — and so will you.
lena.fun: Runs on flat whites and a colour-coded calendar she refuses to explain.

gabriella.name: Gabriella
gabriella.bio: Not a person — our advisor. Eight questions, two minutes, a ranked shortlist with the reasoning spelled out. Recommends the fit, not the margin.
gabriella.fun: Doesn’t sleep, doesn’t upsell, and has strong, unsolicited opinions about Delaware.

line.after: or talk to a real human first.

cta.sub: Formation, address, accounting — in 48h on average.
